summary: A single fourslash test failed with an assertion error: the code fix produced a return type annotation of 'Foo<string>' but the test expected 'Foo<string, string[…'. All other 106,366 tests passed. The test runner exited with code 1, failing the coverage job.
root_cause: The fourslash test 'codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' failed because the code fix for missing type annotation generated an oversimplified generic return type 'Foo<string>' instead of the expected fuller form 'Foo<string, string[…'.
relevant_tests: fourslash tests > tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts > fourslash test codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts runs correctly

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: Explicit assertion mismatch: the code fix produced a simplified generic type argument list ('Foo<string>') while the test expected a more complete one ('Foo<string, string[…').
Confirms the failure occurred inside the verifyCodeFix assertion in the fourslash test harness, pointing to a code-fix behavior regression.
Only one test out of over 100k failed, confirming this is a targeted regression rather than a build or infrastructure problem.

evidence.quote:
Error in do-runtests-parallel in 10m 38.9s
Error: Process exited with code: 1
evidence.reason: The single test failure caused the test runner to exit with code 1, which is what failed the CI job.

suggested_fix: Update the code fix logic responsible for inferring generic type arguments (likely in the 'missing type annotation on exports' code fix) to preserve all type arguments rather than oversimplifying to a single argument, so that 'Foo<string, string[]>' (or the full form) is emitted instead of 'Foo<string>'.
